title: Test pipeline error handling and retry logic with simulated failures
description: Exercise the retry logic, execution logging, and email notifications built under task-011 by deliberately inducing pipeline failures in Fabric, confirming that retries fire, the error log table records the failure, and notifications are delivered. Originally tracked as inline subtask task-011-4 inside task-011, which was marked Finished while this item was still Pending — promoted to a standalone task by /health-check 2026-07-19 so it is visible to routing.
acceptanceCriteria: A deliberately induced activity failure triggers the configured retry policy the expected number of times.
The failure is written to the execution/error log table with a usable error message and timestamp.
The configured notification fires and reaches the intended recipient.
Results recorded in task notes, including any gap between documented and observed retry behavior.
notes: PROMOTED by /health-check 2026-07-19 from task-011's legacy inline subtask array (id was task-011-4). task-011 remains Finished — retry config, execution log table, notifications, and monitoring docs were all built and verified; this was the human-owned Fabric-deployment test tail.

RELATED to the open task-026 error-handler follow-up flagged on the dashboard (pipeline_error_handler wiring was deliberately deferred to a new dedicated task pending a failure-propagation topology + notification-sink decision). If that task gets filed, sequence this test after it so the test exercises the final topology rather than the interim one.
STALENESS CHECK: authored 2026-01; task-026 changes how data-quality checks fail the pipeline, which may change what a realistic induced failure looks like.

BLOCKED BY task-041 (recorded 2026-07-22): criteria 2 and 3 cannot pass as things stand. Criterion 2 asserts the failure is written to the execution/error log table, but gold_pipeline_execution_log is written by nothing — pipeline_error_handler is a complete implementation that no pipeline activity and no notebook invokes (verified with a positive control). Criterion 3 asserts a notification fires, but all three RefreshDataflow activities carry "NoNotification" and the pipeline JSON has no email/Teams/webhook configuration at all. Criterion 1 (retry policy fires the expected number of times) IS testable today and is already partly evidenced: data_quality_checks with retry=1 produced exactly 2 attempts on the 2026-07-22 failing runs. Do not route this task as ready until task-041 lands.
